title: Brock Peters
content:
{{Top|rwp}} {{Person |image=[[File:Brock peters.jpg]] |name=Brock Peters |birth=[[1920s#1927|1927]]
[[Wikipedia:New York City|New York City]]{{WebCite|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2005/08/24/movies/brock-peters-of-to-kill-a-mockingbird-is-dead-at-78.html|text=Brock Peters of 'To Kill a Mockingbird' Is Dead at 78|author=Mel Watkins|date=2005-08-24|work=[[Wikipedia:The New York Times|''The New York Times'']]|archivedate=20230812212022}} |death=[[August 23]], [[2005]] |listedpronouns= |nationality=[[Wikipedia:American|American]] |occupation=*[[Wikipedia:Actor|Actor]] *[[Wikipedia:Singer|Singer]] *[[Wikipedia:Voice acting|Voice artist]] |companies= |website= }} '''Brock Peters''' ([[1920s|1927]] – [[August 23]], [[2005]]) was an [[Wikipedia:American|american]] [[Wikipedia:Actor|actor]], [[Wikipedia:Singer|singer]], and [[Wikipedia:Voice acting|voice artist]]. Peters voiced [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] in the [[Star Wars radio dramas|radio drama]] adaptations of ''[[Star Wars (radio)|Star Wars]]'', ''[[The Empire Strikes Back (radio)|The Empire Strikes Back]]'', and ''[[Return of the Jedi (radio)|Return of the Jedi]]'' in [[1981]], [[1983]], and [[1996]] respectively.[[Star Wars radio dramas]] In [[1997]], Peters voiced Darth Vader in the read-along story books of the ''[[Original trilogy]]''.''[[A New Hope Read-Along]]'' The same recordings were later used in the [[2015]] read-along story books.''[[A New Hope Read-Along Storybook and CD]]'' ==Works== ===Discography=== {|{{Prettytable}} ! Year !! Title !! Contribution(s) !!
